source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 16.2.1 Rules - facilities (pp. 305-307)

questions.q: An rsyslog rule line has two parts. What are they?
options: A selector field (facility.priority) and an action field (where to send matching messages)
A source and a destination IP
questions.why: Each rule is 'selector  action', where the selector is facility.priority and the action says how to handle matches.

options: Logs generated by the Linux kernel (drivers, kernel modules, system exceptions)
questions.why: kern is kernel-generated logs; mail is mail services, cron is scheduled tasks, lpr is printing.

questions.q: What is the difference between the 'auth' and 'authpriv' facilities?
options: authpriv records more SENSITIVE security events (like sudo); auth is general security/authentication
auth is for printers; authpriv is for mail
questions.why: authpriv ('priv' = private) captures sensitive auth events such as sudo; auth is broader security/authentication.

questions.q: In a rule's selector, what does the * character mean (e.g. *.info or mail.*)?
options: All facilities (in the facility position) or all priorities (in the priority position)
questions.why: * matches all facilities or all priorities depending on its position; ',' combines facilities, ';' separates rules.

questions.q: Which facility records the running logs of system daemons (service start, status, errors)?
questions.why: The daemon facility covers system service/daemon logs.
